title: valeriana dioica subsp. simplicifolia
intro: Valeriana dioica subsp. simplicifolia, also known as red valerian or red valerian root, is a subspecies of the Valeriana dioica plant, which belongs to the Valerianaceae family. It is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its reddish-purple flowers and roots that have been used for centuries for their medicinal and therapeutic properties. The roots of Valeriana dioica subsp. simplicifolia contain valerenic acid, a compound that has been shown to have sedative, anxiolytic, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a popular natural remedy for promoting relaxation, reducing stress and anxiety, and improving sleep quality. Valeriana dioica subsp. simplicifolia can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat insomnia, restlessness, and other nervous system disorders.
